redundancy-pay-calculator.question: Do I usually need 2 years with my employer to qualify for statutory redundancy pay?
redundancy-pay-calculator.answer: Yes. GOV.UK says you only qualify for statutory redundancy pay if you have worked for your employer for at least 2 years.

redundancy-pay-calculator.question: Can my contract offer more than the statutory minimum redundancy pay?
redundancy-pay-calculator.answer: Yes. A contract can provide an enhanced redundancy package, so the statutory calculator is a floor, not always the final result.

notice-period-calculator.question: Can my contract give me more notice than the statutory minimum?
notice-period-calculator.answer: Yes. GOV.UK says a contract can give more notice than the statutory minimum, but not less.

notice-period-calculator.question: Does this tool cover every dismissal situation?
notice-period-calculator.answer: No. Misconduct dismissals, payment in lieu of notice, settlement terms, and other facts can change the practical outcome.

holiday-entitlement-calculator.question: How much statutory paid holiday do most workers get?
holiday-entitlement-calculator.answer: Acas says workers are entitled to 5.6 weeks' statutory paid holiday a year, and bank holidays may be included in that total.

holiday-entitlement-calculator.question: What happens to unused statutory holiday when someone leaves a job?
holiday-entitlement-calculator.answer: Acas says an employer must pay in lieu for any untaken statutory holiday entitlement the worker has accrued when they leave.

settlement-agreement-tax-checker.question: Is the first £30,000 of every settlement automatically tax free?
settlement-agreement-tax-checker.answer: No. GOV.UK says the first combined £30,000 may be tax free for certain termination-payment elements such as statutory redundancy pay and additional severance, but PILON, holiday pay and wages are treated differently.

settlement-agreement-tax-checker.question: Do I need independent advice before signing a settlement agreement?
settlement-agreement-tax-checker.answer: Yes. Acas says a settlement agreement must meet legal conditions to be valid, including that the worker gets advice from a relevant independent adviser.

statutory-paternity-pay-calculator.question: How much is Statutory Paternity Pay in 2026 to 2027?
statutory-paternity-pay-calculator.answer: GOV.UK says the weekly rate is £194.32 or 90% of average weekly earnings, whichever is lower.

statutory-paternity-pay-calculator.question: How much statutory paternity leave can an employee take?
statutory-paternity-pay-calculator.answer: Acas says an eligible employee can choose to take 1 or 2 weeks of statutory paternity leave.

statutory-maternity-pay-calculator.question: How much is Statutory Maternity Pay in 2026 to 2027 after the first 6 weeks?
statutory-maternity-pay-calculator.answer: GOV.UK says the weekly rate for the remaining 33 weeks is £194.32 or 90% of average weekly earnings, whichever is lower.

statutory-maternity-pay-calculator.question: How long can Statutory Maternity Pay be paid for?
statutory-maternity-pay-calculator.answer: GOV.UK says Statutory Maternity Pay can be paid for up to 39 weeks.
